title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Compiler Case: Data Minimization
summary: A request to audit a public website names pages to collect but no owner, user decision, permitted purpose, stop contact, privacy boundary, or proof that findings are useful. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts data minimization increment in authorized inclusive site-audit charter. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Define necessary fields, purpose, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk before collection.
Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Predict Data Minimization · Theory Data Minimization
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 6065 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Build Data Minimization evidence · Theory Data Minimization
